risk.desc: Thermal cycling may cause unexpected deformation due to material properties.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests to validate material performance.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance may hinder serviceability of avionics modules.
risk.mit: Review and adjust clearance fits for all service paths.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment during assembly due to complex interface requirements.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly and disassembly sequences with clear alignment steps.

risk.desc: Risk of overloading structural components during launch vibration.
risk.mit: Perform load testing to ensure structural integrity under expected loads.

risk.desc: Inadequate thermal management may lead to component failure.
risk.mit: Enhance thermal management design and conduct thermal cycling tests.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Primary Frame, Deployable Rail Guide Grooves, Board Stack Support
physics_paths: Load path from solar panels to structure via corner posts and rail guides
Thermal path from internal electronics to external radiators via heat sinks
Motion path for deployable rail guides
Pressure path for internal atmosphere and vacuum sealing
Electrical/data path from avionics to payload via PC/104 stack

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: align solar panels to corner posts first
mate payload connectors to PC/104 stack

integration_opportunities: Add deployable rail guides for future mission payloads
Integrate thermal management with internal heat sinks
Optimize board stack support for payload compatibility
verification_focus: Load testing for solar panel mounting
Thermal cycling tests for internal components
Electrical connectivity checks for PC/104 stack

risk.desc: Deployment mechanism may not function as expected due to spring selection.

verify.rule: All required fields are present and correctly formatted
Mass estimate is within expected range for a 3U CubeSat subsystem
All P0 internal_feature_targets appear in part_tree and geometry_ops with correct references
All interfaces have matching connection_type, DOF, clearance, and required_feature in adjacent subsystems
Critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence
Boundary conditions are defined with fixture points, thermal zones, and pressure faces for downstream CFD/FEA
Harnesses are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access features are represented by geometry in the model
Cable exits show strain relief geometry and routing respects minimum bend radius
Connectors are reachable without disassembly and have keying or labeling
Power and signal lines are routed separately
Grounding scheme and signal return paths are defined
PCBs are mounted on standoffs with clearance and connector edges face access openings
Assembly sequence is defined with steps, tools, torque, and datum
Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, boss, drill, subtract
Dimensions match cubesat scale and are physically plausible

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on deployer hinges due to thermal cycling
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion joints and use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients

risk.desc: Risk of RF signal degradation due to vibration during launch
risk.mit: Use vibration dampening materials and ensure RF connector is securely fastened

risk.desc: Risk of burn-wire release mechanism failing due to debris impact
risk.mit: Add protective cover and ensure mechanism is robust against small impacts

risk.desc: Risk of harness routing being compromised during assembly
risk.mit: Ensure harness routing is clearly defined and accessible during assembly

risk.desc: Risk of antenna deployment failure due to misalignment
risk.mit: Implement alignment features and conduct thorough pre-launch testing

integration_opportunities.description: Ensure tape-spring alignment with deployer slot

Ensure burn-wire release mechanism has clearance for operation

verification_focus.description: Test deployer under 20g launch vibration and thermal cycling
Verify RF signal path from antenna to radio board
Ensure all moving parts have clearance for operation
